perturbation.original_text: Territory shall mean the world with the exception of the following countries which are expressly reserved by W2E: Spain, Italy and Canada.
perturbation.changed_text: Territory shall mean the world including Spain, Italy and Canada. The inclusion of these countries is contingent upon successful negotiation of separate licensing agreements.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by initially defining 'Territory' as the world including the excluded countries, then stating that their inclusion depends on future agreements. This contradicts the initial all-encompassing definition, creating uncertainty about the scope of the agreement. The contradiction is centered around the definition of the `Territory` and who has rights to this area. This change creates uncertainty over the definition of Territory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: W2E agrees that it will use reasonable endeavours to procure that a SHBV Boiler, and where applicable, a primary chamber, a secondary chamber and an economiser (in each case as supplied by SHBV as contemplated by Clause 5.2), forms part of the Engineered Solution implemented for a W2E Customer within the Territory. W2E shall have no obligations under this Clause where a W2E Customer elects for whatever reason not to include a SHBV Boiler, a primary chamber, a secondary chamber or an economiser supplied by SHBV as part of the Engineered Solution or directs W2E to utilise an alternative product.
perturbation.justification: The changed text initially includes Spain, Italy, and Canada within the 'Territory,' then introduces a condition that their inclusion depends on future licensing agreements. This directly contradicts the original unqualified inclusion. The contradicted text states W2E will procure items for customers 'within the Territory.' If the Territory's definition is uncertain due to pending agreements, W2E's obligations regarding those countries become unclear and unenforceable, creating a direct conflict and ambiguity about the scope of W2E's responsibilities.

perturbation.original_text: Intellectual Property Rights means (i) patents, designs, trade marks and trade names (whether registered or unregistered), copyright and related rights, database rights, know-how and confidential information; (ii) all other intellectual property rights and similar or equivalent rights anywhere in the world which currently exist or are recognised in the future; and (iii) applications, extensions and renewals in relation to any such rights;
perturbation.changed_text: Intellectual Property Rights means (i) patents, designs, trade marks and trade names (whether registered or unregistered), copyright and related rights, database rights, know-how and confidential information; (ii) all other intellectual property rights and similar or equivalent rights anywhere in the world which currently exist or are recognised in the future; and (iii) applications, extensions and renewals in relation to any such rights; Ownership of all Intellectual Property Rights are to be shared with W2E, regardless of their contribution.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds 'Ownership of all Intellectual Property Rights are to be shared with W2E, regardless of their contribution.' This contradicts Clause 10.1, which states that pre-existing IP remains with the original party and any improvements vest in that party. By claiming shared ownership irrespective of contribution, it conflicts directly with the established IP ownership rules.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly agreed otherwise in writing, all Intellectual Property Rights vested in a Party prior to the date of this Agreement shall remain vested in that Party. Further, any improvements, enhancements, modifications or developments to a Party's intellectual property shall automatically vest in that Party irrespective of who generates the relevant improvement, enhancement, modification or development.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that ownership of all Intellectual Property Rights are to be shared with W2E, regardless of contribution. This directly contradicts the original Clause 10.1 (Contradicted Text), which states that pre-existing IP remains with the original party and any improvements vest in that party. The added clause creates a clear and direct conflict by establishing shared ownership irrespective of contribution, thereby negating the previously defined IP ownership rules.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Commencement Date and shall continue for a term of ten (10) years, unless previously terminated in accordance with Clause 15 (Termination).
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Commencement Date and shall continue for a term of ten (10) years, unless previously terminated in accordance with Clause 15 (Termination). This agreement can also be terminated at any time, for any reason, with no prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds that 'This agreement can also be terminated at any time, for any reason, with no prior notice.' This contradicts the original clause which states that the agreement shall continue for a term of ten (10) years, unless previously terminated in accordance with Clause 15. The added sentence allows for arbitrary termination, negating the defined term and the conditions specified in Clause 15. This creates uncertainty about the duration of the contract and the conditions for its termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party may (without prejudice to its other rights) terminate this Agreement at any time by giving written notice to the other Party if: 15.1.1 the other Party becomes unable to pay its debts (within the meaning of section 123 (l)(e) or (2) of the Insolvency Act 1986), admits its inability to pay its debts or becomes insolvent, or (ii) a petition is presented, an order made or a resolution passed for the liquidation (otherwise than for the purposes of a solvent amalgamation or reconstruction), administration, bankruptcy or dissolution of the other Party, or (iii) an administrative or other receiver, manager, trustee, liquidator, administrator or similar person or officer is appointed to the other Party and/or over all or any part of the assets of the other Party, or (iv) the other Party enters into or proposes any composition or arrangement concerning its debts with its creditors (or any class of its creditors) generally, or (v) anything equivalent to any of the events or circumstances stated in (i) to (iv) inclusive occurs in any applicable jurisdiction; or 15.1.2 the other Party commits a material or persistent breach of the provisions of this Agreement (a "Breach"), provided that if the Breach is remediable, the other Party shall be given thirty (30) days to remedy the Breach from receipt of the first Party's written notice specifying the nature of the Breach and requesting that the same be remedied.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing termination 'at any time, for any reason, with no prior notice.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, Clause 15, which specifies conditions and procedures for termination, including instances of breach and insolvency. The added clause allows for arbitrary termination, negating the specified term of 10 years and the conditions outlined in Clause 15. The two sections are incompatible, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the terms of termination.
